content.text:
* Perform a mutation spike (both backend and frontend), and check the issues found. Then run a full security scan of entire codebase to identify any vulnerabilities [considering the recent security events].
* Then check the docs once [READMEs, github page, progress, etc.] and make sure they are up to date with the latest changes.
* Next deploy the latest changes to prod [full routine pre - post checklist coverage, if needed run the ad-hoc harness job once, but make sure to turn everyting off once the validation is completed.]

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- qa-verifier: QA/QE agent that (1) runs the full local verification battery mirroring CI's required checks before any commit/push/PR, (2) audits test quality on changed code — failure-mode coverage, mocked-seam smells, coverage on changed files (Week-8 ≥80% gate), and (3) exercises the running application with data-level scenarios — authoring suites/checks through the real API, negative and edge-case inputs, authz probes — against the local stack's seeded demo data. Use proactively before pushing a branch or opening a PR, after writing or modifying tests, or when the user asks "run the gate", "is this ready to push?", "is this tested enough?", or "smoke the app with bad data". (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- ui-tester: End-to-end UI QA agent that exercises DataQ's frontend on BOTH desktop and mobile viewports (functionality + rendering) against the running app, AND audits backend↔frontend feature parity — flagging backend capabilities with no frontend surface (and frontend calls with no backend). Use when the user asks to "test the UI", "check mobile rendering", "find backend/frontend gaps", "is this feature wired end-to-end", or before a release. Drives a live browser via the Playwright MCP and reads code for the parity audit. (Tools: All tools)

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service — even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer — your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

hf:// URIs can be converted to browser URLs by replacing hf://buckets/OWNER/NAME/PATH with https://huggingface.co/buckets/OWNER/NAME/resolve/PATH; for models, datasets, and spaces, use https://huggingface.co[/datasets|/spaces]/OWNER/NAME/resolve/main/PATH. ## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# DataQ backend — mutation-testing tool (manual / periodic, NOT CI).
#
# Deliberately standalone: this file is NOT `-r`'d by requirements-dev.txt, so
# mutmut and its transitive deps (libcst, parso, textual, …) stay OFF the CI
# install and the `pip-audit -r requirements-dev.txt` merge gate. Mutation testing
# is a manual, periodic spike (see CONTRIBUTING.md rule 4a) — it never runs in CI —
# so it has no place on the runtime (`requirements.txt`) or audited-dev surface.
# Pinned here only for reproducibility.
#
# 3.x is a ground-up rewrite (config in pyproject `[tool.mutmut]`, copies the tree
# into ./mutants/ to run); it dropped the pony-ORM cache whose decompiler crashed
# `mutmut results`/`show` on Python 3.13 (#253), so reporting works natively again.
#
# Install:  pip install -r backend/requirements-mutation.txt

mutmut==3.6.0

## Week-8 rule-4a mutation spike (go-live checklist item)

Two spikes run 2026-07-03 on this week's new/core code:

### mutmut — `backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py`
**436/436 mutants killed, 0 survived** (`mutmut results` empty), driven by `tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py` (31 tests incl. the parametrized health-score/pass-rate/state batteries + the #560 delta/duration tests). No action needed; recorded as the strongest module measured so far.

### Stryker — `frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts`
**Mutation score 82.35%** (84 killed · **14 survived** · **4 no-coverage** · 0 timeout/error).

Surviving class (from the clear-text report): **StringLiteral mutations in the status→antd-colour maps** — e.g. `running: 'processing'`, `cancelled: 'warning'` (RUN_STATUS_COLORS) and `pass: 'success'`, `fail: 'error'`, `skip: 'default'` (RESULT_STATUS_COLORS) each mutate to `""` with all tests green. The vitest suite exercises the formatters (`formatDuration(Ms)`, `formatScalar`, `formatTimestamp`, window logic) but treats the colour maps as data nobody asserts — a wrong badge colour for any status would ship silently. 4 mutants sit on uncovered lines.

## Suggested fix
- Table-driven assertion over every entry of `RUN_STATUS_COLORS` / `RESULT_STATUS_COLORS` / `RUN_BAR_STATUS` / `pipelineStatusColor` (exact expected antd colour per status — these are UI contract, not incidental data), which also picks up the 4 no-coverage lines.

# Frontend mutation testing (Stryker)

The frontend analogue of the backend mutmut spike ([CONTRIBUTING rule 4a](../../CONTRIBUTING.md)).
**Manual / periodic, never CI** — mutation testing is too slow to gate merges, and
its big tool tree shouldn't sit on the `pnpm audit` surface. So, like the backend's
standalone `requirements-mutation.txt`, **Stryker is not a dependency in
`frontend/package.json`** — [`run.sh`](./run.sh) installs it ad-hoc (pinned) and
restores the manifest on exit.

## Run a spike

```bash
frontend/mutation/run.sh                                  # mutate the configured target
frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/checks/checkForm.ts'
```

Config lives in [`../stryker.conf.json`](../stryker.conf.json); the version pin is in
`run.sh`. The default `mutate` target is `suiteTarget.ts` — point it at whatever pure
module you're hardening (the conversion/resolver utils are the best targets:
`suiteTarget.ts`, `checkForm.ts`, `expectationCatalog.ts`, `resultsFormat.ts`).

## Reading the result

Stryker prints a `[Survived]` block per mutant the tests failed to kill, plus a
mutation-score table. Triage each survivor:

- **Real gap** — a behavioural mutation (flipped condition, dropped branch, changed
  return) that no test caught. Add/strengthen a test. This is the point of the spike.
- **Equivalent mutant** — a change with no observable effect (e.g. mutating a value
  that's re-narrowed downstream, or an unreachable branch). Not a gap; leave it.
- **Low-value** — e.g. an error-`message` string mutated to `""`. Pin it only if the
  copy is contractual; asserting exact UI strings is usually brittle.

A score < 100% is fine — chase the _real_ gaps, not the number.

## Gotchas (Node ≥ 20, pnpm v11)

- Stryker must run from `frontend/` — it sandboxes the cwd, so a separate manifest
  dir can't host it. `run.sh` cd's there for you.
- `stryker.conf.json` sets `plugins: ["@stryker-mutator/vitest-runner"]` explicitly;

content.content:
125	# ── mutmut (mutation spikes) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
126	# Manual/periodic, NOT a CI gate (CONTRIBUTING rule 4a). mutmut 3.x copies the
127	# tree into ./mutants/, chdir's in, and runs pytest there — so `also_copy` must
128	# bring the whole importable package + tests + this config, and `-o addopts=`
129	# strips the repo's --cov flags (mutmut runs its own coverage plugin; a second
130	# --cov is a pytest usage error in-process). Per-spike: point `source_paths` at
131	# the one module you're spiking and narrow the test selection to its tests.

# tree into ./mutants/, chdir's in, and runs pytest there — so `also_copy` must
# bring the whole importable package + tests + this config, and `-o addopts=`
# strips the repo's --cov flags (mutmut runs its own coverage plugin; a second
# --cov is a pytest usage error in-process). Per-spike: point `source_paths` at
# the one module you're spiking and narrow the test selection to its tests.
[tool.mutmut]
source_paths = ["backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py"]
also_copy = ["backend", "pyproject.toml"]
pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection = ["backend/tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py"]
pytest_add_cli_args = ["-o", "addopts="]

input.description: Show Stryker run script

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Frontend mutation spike (Stryker) — manual / periodic, NOT CI.
#
# Mirrors the backend mutmut spike (CONTRIBUTING.md rule 4a): a hostile pass over
# a pure module to find covered-but-unasserted logic. Stryker + its vitest runner
# are deliberately NOT in frontend/package.json, so they stay off the `pnpm audit`
# merge gate (the backend keeps mutmut off `pip-audit` the same way via a
# standalone requirements-mutation.txt). This script installs them ad-hoc, runs
# Stryker from the project root (Stryker sandboxes the cwd, so it must run there),
# and restores the manifest on exit — even if Stryker fails — so the mutation deps
# never land in the committed package.json / lockfile.
#
# Usage (from anywhere):
#   frontend/mutation/run.sh                         # mutate the configured target
#   frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/checks/checkForm.ts'
# Pin lives here so spikes are reproducible:
STRYKER_VERSION=9.6.1

set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."   # -> frontend/ (Stryker must run from the project root)

# Refuse to run with a dirty manifest — the EXIT trap restores it via `git
# checkout`, which would clobber any unrelated staged/working changes to it.
if ! git diff --quiet -- package.json pnpm-lock.yaml ||
   ! git diff --cached --quiet -- package.json pnpm-lock.yaml; then
  echo "✗ package.json / pnpm-lock.yaml have uncommitted changes — commit or stash them first" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Restore the manifest no matter how Stryker exits (keeps the spike deps uncommitted).
trap 'git checkout -- package.json pnpm-lock.yaml 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT

# typescript is already a project dep; Stryker resolves it from node_modules.
# Explicit plugin name in stryker.conf.json — pnpm's strict layout defeats the
# default "@stryker-mutator/*" glob plugin discovery.
pnpm add -D "@stryker-mutator/core@${STRYKER_VERSION}" \
            "@stryker-mutator/vitest-runner@${STRYKER_VERSION}"

pnpm exec stryker run "$@"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=83.0.0"]
build-backend = "setuptools.backends.legacy:build"

# ── Black ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.black]
line-length = 100
target-version = ["py313"]
include = '\.pyi?$'

# ── Ruff ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py313"
src = ["backend"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = [
    "E",   # pycodestyle errors
    "W",   # pycodestyle warnings
    "F",   # pyflakes
    "I",   # isort
    "B",   # flake8-bugbear
    "C4",  # flake8-comprehensions
    "UP",  # pyupgrade
    "N",   # pep8-naming
    "S",   # flake8-bandit (security — basic subset; full scan via Bandit)
    "RUF", # ruff-specific rules
]
ignore = [
    "S101",  # assert statements are fine in tests
    "S104",  # binding to 0.0.0.0 is intentional in Docker
    "B008",  # FastAPI Depends() in function args is idiomatic
]
# The format-on-save hook runs `ruff check --fix`. Auto-deleting unused imports
# (F401) mid-edit — when an import is briefly unused between adding it and adding
# its first use — corrupts multi-step edits. Keep F401 *reported* (so genuinely
# unused imports still fail CI) but never auto-removed. Same for unused locals.
unfixable = ["F401", "F841"]

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"backend/tests/**" = ["S", "N802"]  # relax security + naming in tests
"backend/alembic/**" = ["N999"]     # migration files use non-PEP8 names

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
known-first-party = ["app"]

# ── mypy ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.13"
strict = true
warn_return_any = true
warn_unused_configs = true
ignore_missing_imports = false
plugins = ["sqlalchemy.ext.mypy.plugin", "pydantic.mypy"]

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "great_expectations.*",
    "celery.*",
    "snowflake.*",
    "databricks.*",
    "azure.storage.*",
    "azure.core.*",
    "azure.identity",
    "azure.keyvault.*",
    "boto3.*",
    "botocore.*",
    "pyiceberg.*",
    "pandas",
    "pandas.*",
    "croniter",
]
ignore_missing_imports = true
# These third-party libs expose public API via internal modules without an explicit
# __all__ (e.g. great_expectations.expectations.ExpectColumnValuesToNotBeNull), so
# strict mode's no-implicit-reexport would flag legitimate imports/attribute access.
implicit_reexport = true

# pyarrow ships a partial py.typed, so when it's installed (dev env) mypy flags
# its untyped calls (e.g. ParquetFile), but it's absent from the slim CI
# typecheck deps. follow_imports = "skip" forces it to Any in *both* envs, so the
# code type-checks identically with or without pyarrow installed.
# Same treatment for the OTel span stack (tracing.py, A3), openlineage-python
# (lineage/emitter.py, #758), and numpy (datasources/comparison.py, #799): all
# typed (py.typed) but absent from the slim typecheck deps, so forcing them to
# Any keeps local + CI type-checking identical.
[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = ["pyarrow", "pyarrow.*", "numpy", "numpy.*", "opentelemetry.*", "azure.monitor.*", "openlineage.*"]
follow_imports = "skip"
ignore_missing_imports = true

# ── pytest ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
testpaths = ["backend/tests"]
# The Week-8 exit gate: the run fails if backend/app line coverage drops below
# 80% (baseline at flip time: 96%). Applies to every pytest run, local and CI.
# Use --no-cov for a quick single-file loop.
addopts = "--cov=backend/app --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=80"
markers = [
    "real_dispatch: opt out of the autouse run_dispatch stub (test dispatch_run itself)",
]
# Silence UPSTREAM deprecations we can't fix here — scoped by message so a new warning
# from our own code still surfaces. All resolve on a dependency upgrade; none are DataQ
# bugs. (Our one own use of the deprecated Starlette 422 constant was fixed in
# core/errors.py; the two opencensus datetime/locale ignores were dropped with the
# opencensus→OTel log migration, #524.)
filterwarnings = [
    "ignore:Using .httpx. with .starlette.testclient. is deprecated",                  # fastapi TestClient
    "ignore:Implicitly cleaning up <TemporaryDirectory:ResourceWarning",               # great_expectations ephemeral context
]

# ── Bandit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.bandit]
targets = ["backend/app"]
exclude_dirs = ["backend/tests", "backend/alembic"]
skips = ["B101"]  # assert is fine in non-production code

# ── mutmut (mutation spikes) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Manual/periodic, NOT a CI gate (CONTRIBUTING rule 4a). mutmut 3.x copies the
# tree into ./mutants/, chdir's in, and runs pytest there — so `also_copy` must
# bring the whole importable package + tests + this config, and `-o addopts=`
# strips the repo's --cov flags (mutmut runs its own coverage plugin; a second
# --cov is a pytest usage error in-process). Per-spike: point `source_paths` at
# the one module you're spiking and narrow the test selection to its tests.
[tool.mutmut]
source_paths = ["backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py"]
also_copy = ["backend", "pyproject.toml"]
pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection = ["backend/tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py"]
pytest_add_cli_args = ["-o", "addopts="]

content.content:
"""Shared fixtures for the lineage tests.

Env hygiene: every test starts with the OpenLineage transport vars unset and the
cached client cleared, so tests can't leak configuration into each other (a stray
``OPENLINEAGE_URL`` would otherwise flip the whole suite out of the dark path).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Iterator

import pytest

from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
from backend.app.lineage import emitter

_OL_ENV_VARS = (
    "OPENLINEAGE_URL",
    "OPENLINEAGE__TRANSPORT__TYPE",
    "OPENLINEAGE_CONFIG",
    "OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED",
    # Lineage catalog-pull gate (#762) — cleared too so a stray LINEAGE_PROVIDER /
    # MARQUEZ_URL can't flip the pull-provider factory out of the dark path mid-suite.
    "LINEAGE_PROVIDER",
    "MARQUEZ_URL",
)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _clean_openlineage_env(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> Iterator[None]:
    for var in _OL_ENV_VARS:
        monkeypatch.delenv(var, raising=False)
    # The gate reads typed Settings (lru_cache'd) — drop any instance built before
    # the delenv so this test starts genuinely dark. The client cache mirrors it.
    get_settings.cache_clear()
    emitter.reset_openlineage_client_cache()
    yield

"""Shared fixtures for the lineage tests.

Env hygiene: every test starts with the OpenLineage transport vars unset and the
cached client cleared, so tests can't leak configuration into each other (a stray
``OPENLINEAGE_URL`` would otherwise flip the whole suite out of the dark path).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Iterator

import pytest

from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
from backend.app.lineage import emitter

_OL_ENV_VARS = (
    "OPENLINEAGE_URL",
    "OPENLINEAGE__TRANSPORT__TYPE",
    "OPENLINEAGE_CONFIG",
    "OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED",
    # Lineage catalog-pull gate (#762) — cleared too so a stray LINEAGE_PROVIDER /
    # MARQUEZ_URL can't flip the pull-provider factory out of the dark path mid-suite.
    "LINEAGE_PROVIDER",
    "MARQUEZ_URL",
)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _clean_openlineage_env(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> Iterator[None]:
    for var in _OL_ENV_VARS:
        monkeypatch.delenv(var, raising=False)
    # The gate reads typed Settings (lru_cache'd) — drop any instance built before
    # the delenv so this test starts genuinely dark. The client cache mirrors it.
    get_settings.cache_clear()
    emitter.reset_openlineage_client_cache()
    yield
    emitter.reset_openlineage_client_cache()

file.content:
# Resolution order for the test DB:
#   1. TEST_DATABASE_URL if set explicitly (this is what CI does).
#   2. Otherwise, the docker-compose Postgres using the .env creds, on a dedicated
#      `dataq_test` database (auto-created if missing). This is what makes a plain
#      `pytest` — including editors like VS Code / PyCharm whose test runners invoke
#      pytest directly, NOT via scripts/test-backend.sh — run the DB-backed tests
#      instead of skipping, whenever the local Postgres is up.
#   3. Neither available → the db_session fixture skips, so `pytest` still runs the
#      pure-unit suite anywhere.


def _read_env_file() -> dict[str, str]:
    """Best-effort parse of the gitignored repo-root .env (the POSTGRES_* creds that
    docker-compose + scripts/setup.sh use). Returns {} if it's absent."""
    from pathlib import Path

    env: dict[str, str] = {}
    path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / ".env"
    if path.exists():
        for raw in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = raw.strip()
            if line and not line.startswith("#") and "=" in line:
                key, _, val = line.partition("=")
                env[key.strip()] = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
    return env


def _resolve_test_database_url() -> str | None:
    explicit = os.environ.get("TEST_DATABASE_URL")
    if explicit:
        return explicit
    env = _read_env_file()
    user, password = env.get("POSTGRES_USER"), env.get("POSTGRES_PASSWORD")
    if not (user and password):
        return None
    return f"REDACTED"


def _ensure_local_test_database() -> None:
    """When we defaulted to the local `dataq_test` DB (TEST_DATABASE_URL unset),
    create it if missing — so a direct `pytest` works with only the compose Postgres
    up, no manual createdb. No-op when TEST_DATABASE_URL is set explicitly (CI: the
    DB is provisioned by the workflow)."""
    if os.environ.get("TEST_DATABASE_URL"):
        return
    env = _read_env_file()
    user, password, admin_db = (
        env.get("POSTGRES_USER"),
        env.get("POSTGRES_PASSWORD"),
        env.get("POSTGRES_DB"),
    )
    if not (user and password and admin_db):
        return
    from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text

    admin_url = f"REDACTED"
    try:
        # AUTOCOMMIT: CREATE DATABASE can't run inside a transaction.
        admin = create_engine(admin_url, future=True, isolation_level="AUTOCOMMIT")
        with admin.connect() as conn:
            exists = conn.execute(
                text("SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = 'dataq_test'")
            ).scalar()
            if not exists:
                conn.execute(text("CREATE DATABASE dataq_test"))
        admin.dispose()
    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - environment-dependent
        pass  # Postgres down / no perms — _db_engine's connect below skips cleanly.


TEST_DATABASE_URL = _resolve_test_database_url()
# If we defaulted to the local compose Postgres, export it so per-test skipif guards
# that read os.environ['TEST_DATABASE_URL'] directly (e.g. the custom-SQL GX tests)
# also run — not only the db_session fixture. setdefault → CI's explicit value wins.
# We deliberately do NOT set DATABASE_URL / REDIS_URL here, so the real-infra E2E

input.description: Credential-leak security audit
input.prompt:
You are doing a focused security audit of the DataQ repo at /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (backend: FastAPI+Celery, backend/app/...). Context: two recent prod security incidents set the lens:
1. #849: PATs were logged in plaintext because a DEPENDENCY (fastapi_azure_auth) logged the raw bearer on failed JWT decode. Fix (#851): PAT never reaches the validator + a logger-level scrub of bare credentials in backend/app/core/logging.py.
2. #852: the OTel exporter's own azure.core HTTP logs re-entered the log bridge (amplification loop) and starved Celery. Fix: a filter on the export handler + propagate=False on azure.core.

Your job — verify the fixes are robust and hunt for OTHER instances of the same classes across the entire backend:
- Find every third-party library in requirements.txt that could log request headers, URLs with credentials (SAS tokens in query strings!), connection strings, or payloads at INFO/DEBUG/WARNING on failure paths (azure SDKs, snowflake-connector, databricks, requests/urllib3, botocore, openlineage, celery, kombu). Check whether the logging pipeline (backend/app/core/logging.py) scrubbing/filters would catch a credential emitted by ANY logger, not just our own — i.e., is the scrub attached at the handler/root level or only to structlog?
- Check OTel span attributes (backend/app/core/tracing.py) for anything that could carry credentials/PII (URLs with SAS query strings, db connection strings).
- Check exception paths that serialize exception text into DB columns or API responses (e.g. connections' last_poll_error is supposed to be CLASSIFIED never raw — verify nothing else stores raw exception text that could carry a SAS/PAT: run failure_reason, incident records, alerting payloads, lineage refresh errors in warehouse_refresh.py / warehouse.py).
- Check the loop-breaker logger list covers all azure.* loggers that log HTTP traffic (azure.core.pipeline.policies.http_logging_policy etc.) and that the export-handler filter can't be defeated by a level reset.
- Check tracebacks/locals can't leak (the #536 class — traceback-locals credential leak).

Report ONLY concrete findings with file:line references, each with: severity (high/med/low), what leaks/fails, how to trigger it, suggested fix. If a class is verified clean, say so in one line. Return raw findings text, no preamble.

input.description: Injection/SSRF/authz security audit
input.prompt:
You are doing a focused security audit of the DataQ repo at /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (backend: FastAPI+Celery under backend/app/, frontend React under frontend/src/). Audit these three surfaces for the ENTIRE codebase and report concrete findings only:

1. SQL injection: backend/app/datasources/sql.py holds the shared SQL-identifier allowlist (#428). Verify EVERY place that interpolates identifiers or values into SQL strings goes through it — especially the NEW warehouse-native lineage providers (backend/app/lineage/warehouse_snowflake.py, warehouse_unity_catalog.py — do their queries interpolate connection-supplied values like account/database/schema/watermark timestamps?), services/dataset_reader.py, services/schema_drift.py (both have nosec B608 pragmas — verify the justification holds), datasources/monitors.py, services/profiler. Watermark/timestamp interpolation into WHERE clauses counts.

2. SSRF + webhook egress: webhook URL validation (the SSRF guard shared by alerting Teams/Slack and the HealthPublisher path) — verify scheme + host checks are consistent everywhere outbound requests are made with user-configurable URLs (alerting/*.py, orchestration providers' poll URLs, marquez.py, openlineage emitter, Unity Catalog workspace URL, dbt artifact store). Any http:// allowed where https should be forced? Any redirect-following that could bypass the guard?

3. AuthZ: the assets/lineage/incidents endpoints follow the ONE-RULE redaction model (ADR 0034 amended — neighbours outside your grants are redacted-not-omitted; suite-less assets visible to all). Verify backend/app/api/v1/assets.py + incidents.py + the graph/browse/detail paths all enforce grants consistently, verify the admin gates (require_workspace_admin), the MCP tools (backend/app/mcp/) enforce the same suite-scoped authz as REST, and rate limiting (backend/app/core/rate_limit.py) can't be bypassed via header spoofing given XFF trusted-hops=3.

For each finding: severity, file:line, the exploit path, suggested fix. If a surface is verified clean, one line saying so. Raw findings text, no preamble.

input.description: Docs staleness audit
input.prompt:
Audit the DataQ repo docs for staleness against the current state of main (repo: /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ). Recent merges to check coverage for: warehouse-native lineage #858 (PRs #886/#887/#890/#891 — Snowflake + Unity Catalog lineage providers, warehouse-lineage beat, per-connection watermark/health, tier/degraded/failing surfaced on the asset graph), ADR 0036 (connection-anchored check engines, #894), and the ledger commits #896/#897.

Check:
1. README.md (repo root) — feature list / architecture claims current?
2. docs/progress.md — does the ledger reflect the latest merges (#884–#897)? Open-PR count accurate (should be 0 per last commit)? Snapshot counts consistent?
3. docs/adr/README.md index — includes ADR 0036 and all prior ADRs with correct statuses?
4. docs/architecture.md — does the diagram/text mention warehouse-native lineage where relevant?
5. docs/feature-matrix.md — lineage tiers (dbt-manifest vs warehouse-native per connection type) reflected?
6. docs/orchestration.md, docs/runbook-faq.md — still consistent with the #839/#841 poll-health + alerting behaviour?
7. CLAUDE.md §13 headline — spot obvious contradictions with the ledger (do NOT rewrite CLAUDE.md, just report).
8. frontend/backend user docs (docs/user/ if present) — asset graph tier/degraded/failing badges documented?
9. CONTRIBUTING.md rule references still accurate where docs cite rule numbers.

Do NOT make any edits. Return a list of concrete staleness findings (file, what's stale, what it should say), each tagged must-fix (factually wrong) vs nice-to-have (incomplete). If everything checks out for an area, say so in one line.

257:### Pre-deploy checklist
258-
259-Confirm the change is *ready and green* before you push it to prod:
260-
261-- [ ] **Everything intended is merged to `main`** and you're deploying that known SHA (blank
262-  `image_tag` → the immutable commit SHA).
263-- [ ] **CI is fully green** on that SHA — lint, format, type-check, **all unit + integration
264-  tests**, the security scans (Bandit / CodeQL / secret-scan / dependency-audit), and the
265-  frontend E2E. The local verification battery (the same gate) passes too — don't let CI be
266-  the first feedback loop.
267-- [ ] **Docs are up to date** for what's shipping — `CLAUDE.md` §13 headline + `docs/progress.md`
268-  ticked; an **ADR** for any significant decision; the **env-var reference** + this deploy
269-  guide for any new config; and **user docs** for any new user-facing feature.
270-- [ ] **DB migrations are safe** — additive/backward-compatible (nullable `ADD COLUMN` / new
271-  table; no `DROP`/rename/`NOT NULL`-without-default in the same PR as the code that needs
272-  it), `upgrade` **and** `downgrade` tested locally. The workflow runs `alembic upgrade head`
273-  **before** rolling the apps, so old code never sees a missing column — you just confirm the
274-  revision is safe. *Note:* an `ALTER` on a hot table (`runs` / `results` / `pipeline_runs`)
275-  can block on a live-worker lock and hang the migrate job (#605); recovery + root-cause
276-  hardening are in [#708](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/708).
277-- [ ] **Config + secrets are in place** — the required GitHub env/vars and Key Vault secrets
278-  (see the [prerequisites](#before-you-deploy-production-prerequisites) above), especially any
279-  new key this release reads.

283-After the workflow is green, confirm the app is actually **healthy and fully functional** —

285-
286-- [ ] **It's up & reachable** — `GET /healthz` → 200; the SPA root and a deep link load.
287-- [ ] **A user can sign in** — complete the Azure AD SSO flow end-to-end and land on the

input.description: Read rest of post-deploy smoke checklist

content.content:
- [ ] **A user can sign in** — complete the Azure AD SSO flow end-to-end and land on the
  dashboard as a real user (not just the login screen).
- [ ] **The UI renders correctly** — walk the key pages (Dashboard, Connections, Suites,
  Results, Profile, Admin) and confirm they render with data and **no console / network
  errors**, on **desktop *and* mobile** viewports. (The `ui-tester` agent automates this.)
- [ ] **Every high-level capability works end-to-end** — spot-check the core flows, e.g.:
  add/edit a connection and **test** it; author a check; **trigger a run** and see live
  progress → results → (redacted) failing samples; view dashboard trends; a schedule; an
  alert delivered; the **MCP tools** answer for an AI client. If a release touched a specific
  area, exercise that area harder.
- [ ] **Auth + guards hold** — unauthenticated API and MCP requests are rejected (`401`), and
  the prod-docs gate is on (`/docs`, `/redoc`, `/openapi.json` → `404`, #170).
- [ ] **Infra rolled cleanly** — api / worker / frontend are on the **deployed tag** (not the
  old image), the migrate job execution is `Succeeded`, Celery beat starts clean (#405/#407)
  and orchestration polling reads Key Vault (#406/#408), and App Insights shows no post-roll
  errors.
- [ ] **(Optional, deeper)** run the live-smoke lane (`frontend/e2e-live/` gated on
  `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` + `e2e_smoke.py` `DATAQ_BEARER` mode, #531) and the MCP 4-query protocol
  smoke for an authenticated end-to-end pass.

**Quick reachability + auth probes** (no token — a `401` *is* the pass for an auth-gated
route; everything is the public **frontend** host since the api has no public ingress, ADR
0028 §5):

```bash
FE=https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "healthz         %{http_code}\n"        $FE/healthz       # 200
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "api (auth)      %{http_code}\n"        $FE/api/v1/me     # 401
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "mcp GET         %{http_code}\n"        $FE/mcp/          # 401  NOT 421
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "mcp POST        %{http_code}\n" -X POST -H "content-type: application/json" $FE/mcp/  # 401  NOT 421
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "openapi (gated) %{http_code}\n"        $FE/openapi.json  # 404

# api + worker rolled to the deployed tag?
az containerapp revision list -n dataq-app-api    -g dataq-rg --query "[?properties.active].properties.template.containers[0].image" -o tsv
az containerapp revision list -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --query "[?properties.active].properties.template.containers[0].image" -o tsv
```

> A `421 Misdirected Request` on `/mcp/` (instead of `401`) is a specific known failure —
> FastMCP's DNS-rebind **Host guard** rejecting the nginx-proxied Host (regressed by the
> fastmcp 3.4.3 bump, fixed via `build_mcp_app(allowed_hosts=…)`; see
> [#706](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/706)).

## Operational notes

- **Restart dependent Container Apps after a shared-Postgres delete/recreate** —
  the DB host is injected as a start-time secret snapshot, so every dependent
  revision must be restarted or it keeps resolving the old/dead host.
- The shared RG / Container Apps env / Postgres server are **reused, never
  destroyed** (free/trial caps one of each; shared with the harness — ADR 0024).
- `key_vault_purge_protection` is off during bring-up (so a destroy/re-apply can
  reuse the vault name); set it **true** for a hardened prod (irreversible).
  **Decision (2026-07-02): deliberately left off** for this deployment — the vault
  is demo/trial-scoped and destroy/re-apply flexibility wins; every secret it holds
  (PATs, SAS, webhook secrets) is rotatable, so accidental-delete recovery is

12	email same day. Verified via `az rest .../subscriptions/{id}?api-version=2022-12-01`:
13	`.state == "Disabled"` (`quotaId: FreeTrial_2014-09-01`, `spendingLimit: On` — no PAYG

17	apps) shows `provisioningState: Failed`; the live frontend URL times out completely (no
18	response, not even a 401). **Gotcha:** `az account show` / `az account list` still
19	reported `state: Enabled` — those two commands are not reliable for judging subscription
20	health; use the `az rest` subscription-resource check above, or just try to reach a
21	resource directly.
22	
23	**How to apply:** treat DataQ's Azure deployment as gone until the user decides to either
24	(a) add a payment method in the portal to convert to PAYG and restore, or (b) accept this
25	as the local-first cutover (#590/#591) landing ~2 weeks early. Don't propose Azure-
26	dependent work (live smoke tests, prod deploys, KV secret reads, the harness wake
27	runbook) without first re-checking subscription state via the `az rest` call — it may
28	still be Disabled. As of 2026-07-12 the user has only asked for a status check, no
29	restore/wind-down action taken yet. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] and

36	off before the hard disablement):
37	
38	Decision (2026-07-04): **no pay-as-you-go upgrade** — DataQ wraps up its Azure presence by
39	the free-trial day-30 mark (**~2026-07-26**; W3 milestone due 2026-07-25 is the hard
40	deadline for #590 teardown + #591 local-first posture). Snowflake subscription lapses
41	separately ~2026-07-05..10 (v1.1 W1, #588).
42	
43	**Why:** Cost Management actuals (billing currency **CAD**): burn was ~21.7/day
44	(harness = 80% — Airflow scheduler 8.99 + 2×2-vCPU workers 6.92/day); CAD 144.80 of the
45	free credit already consumed by Jul 4 → credit would have died ~Jul 9–10. Postgres
46	B1ms/32GB is $0 (12-month free SKU). App-only burn ~CAD 4.5/day.
47	
48	**State since 2026-07-04:** the 4 harness container apps (`dataq-harness-airflow`,
49	`-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis`) are **Stopped**; both ADF triggers
50	(`tr_customers_daily`, `tr_orders_landed`) **Stopped**; 5 `mockdata-*` cron jobs
51	**suspended** (api-version `2024-08-02-preview`). The 4 `dataq-app-*` apps still run.
52	Flows B/C dead until woken; DataQ's 10-min Airflow poll logs failures (noise only).
53	**Wake runbook is scripted: `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh`**
54	(`status|start|run [--adf]|stop|window [--adf]`; ~CAD 17/day ≈ 0.70/hour while awake;

59	#590. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] for the reversal state (fresh 25-day
60	PAT; schedules/bindings restore still pending — needs dataq-admin).

file.content:
---
name: project-azure-winddown
description: "Azure free-trial credit exhausted — subscription DISABLED 2026-07-12 (~2 weeks earlier than the planned ~2026-07-26 wind-down); all dataq-rg resources down"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 0aef84ae-9324-4eaf-a425-b6451fedf029
---

**UPDATE 2026-07-12: the subscription is now DISABLED — the wind-down happened on its
own, early and hard.** User got the "$200 credit used up, account and services disabled"
email same day. Verified via `az rest .../subscriptions/{id}?api-version=2022-12-01`:
`.state == "Disabled"` (`quotaId: FreeTrial_2014-09-01`, `spendingLimit: On` — no PAYG
upgrade, so the spending limit hard-stopped everything instead of billing). Confirmed
dead, not a stale CLI cache: Postgres `dataq-pg-wus3-3erlgd` shows `state: Disabled`;
every Container App in `dataq-rg` (dataq-app-api/worker/frontend/redis + the 3 harness
apps) shows `provisioningState: Failed`; the live frontend URL times out completely (no
response, not even a 401). **Gotcha:** `az account show` / `az account list` still
reported `state: Enabled` — those two commands are not reliable for judging subscription
health; use the `az rest` subscription-resource check above, or just try to reach a
resource directly.

**How to apply:** treat DataQ's Azure deployment as gone until the user decides to either
(a) add a payment method in the portal to convert to PAYG and restore, or (b) accept this
as the local-first cutover (#590/#591) landing ~2 weeks early. Don't propose Azure-
dependent work (live smoke tests, prod deploys, KV secret reads, the harness wake
runbook) without first re-checking subscription state via the `az rest` call — it may
still be Disabled. As of 2026-07-12 the user has only asked for a status check, no
restore/wind-down action taken yet. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] and
[[project-dbt-lineage-harness]] for what lived on this subscription.

---

Original 2026-07-04 wind-down plan (superseded above, kept for the cost-actuals context —
the harness stop/mockdata-suspend steps below are accurate history of what was already
off before the hard disablement):

Decision (2026-07-04): **no pay-as-you-go upgrade** — DataQ wraps up its Azure presence by
the free-trial day-30 mark (**~2026-07-26**; W3 milestone due 2026-07-25 is the hard
deadline for #590 teardown + #591 local-first posture). Snowflake subscription lapses
separately ~2026-07-05..10 (v1.1 W1, #588).

**Why:** Cost Management actuals (billing currency **CAD**): burn was ~21.7/day
(harness = 80% — Airflow scheduler 8.99 + 2×2-vCPU workers 6.92/day); CAD 144.80 of the
free credit already consumed by Jul 4 → credit would have died ~Jul 9–10. Postgres
B1ms/32GB is $0 (12-month free SKU). App-only burn ~CAD 4.5/day.

**State since 2026-07-04:** the 4 harness container apps (`dataq-harness-airflow`,
`-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis`) are **Stopped**; both ADF triggers
(`tr_customers_daily`, `tr_orders_landed`) **Stopped**; 5 `mockdata-*` cron jobs
**suspended** (api-version `2024-08-02-preview`). The 4 `dataq-app-*` apps still run.
Flows B/C dead until woken; DataQ's 10-min Airflow poll logs failures (noise only).
**Wake runbook is scripted: `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh`**
(`status|start|run [--adf]|stop|window [--adf]`; ~CAD 17/day ≈ 0.70/hour while awake;
`--adf` needs live Snowflake). Also mirrored as a #590 comment.
**Snowflake timeline corrected 2026-07-04:** the trial ends ~2026-07-25 (21 days,
$384 credits left) — same window as Azure, NOT "within days". The #588 retirement was
executed then reversed the same day; the real retirement re-runs at actual lapse with
#590. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] for the reversal state (fresh 25-day
PAT; schedules/bindings restore still pending — needs dataq-admin).

**How to apply:** don't treat stopped harness apps / failing Airflow polls as incidents;
before any live E2E, wake per the runbook and stop again after. `az` CLI: was left logged
in as demo user Olivia after go-live; owner login is royarijit04@outlook.com (relevant to

toolUseResult.originalFile:
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=83.0.0"]
build-backend = "setuptools.backends.legacy:build"

# ── Black ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.black]
line-length = 100
target-version = ["py313"]
include = '\.pyi?$'

# ── Ruff ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py313"
src = ["backend"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = [
    "E",   # pycodestyle errors
    "W",   # pycodestyle warnings
    "F",   # pyflakes
    "I",   # isort
    "B",   # flake8-bugbear
    "C4",  # flake8-comprehensions
    "UP",  # pyupgrade
    "N",   # pep8-naming
    "S",   # flake8-bandit (security — basic subset; full scan via Bandit)
    "RUF", # ruff-specific rules
]
ignore = [
    "S101",  # assert statements are fine in tests
    "S104",  # binding to 0.0.0.0 is intentional in Docker
    "B008",  # FastAPI Depends() in function args is idiomatic
]
# The format-on-save hook runs `ruff check --fix`. Auto-deleting unused imports
# (F401) mid-edit — when an import is briefly unused between adding it and adding
# its first use — corrupts multi-step edits. Keep F401 *reported* (so genuinely
# unused imports still fail CI) but never auto-removed. Same for unused locals.
unfixable = ["F401", "F841"]

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"backend/tests/**" = ["S", "N802"]  # relax security + naming in tests
"backend/alembic/**" = ["N999"]     # migration files use non-PEP8 names

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
known-first-party = ["app"]

# ── mypy ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.13"
strict = true
warn_return_any = true
warn_unused_configs = true
ignore_missing_imports = false
plugins = ["sqlalchemy.ext.mypy.plugin", "pydantic.mypy"]

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "great_expectations.*",
    "celery.*",
    "snowflake.*",
    "databricks.*",
    "azure.storage.*",
    "azure.core.*",
    "azure.identity",
    "azure.keyvault.*",
    "boto3.*",
    "botocore.*",
    "pyiceberg.*",
    "pandas",
    "pandas.*",
    "croniter",
]
ignore_missing_imports = true
# These third-party libs expose public API via internal modules without an explicit
# __all__ (e.g. great_expectations.expectations.ExpectColumnValuesToNotBeNull), so
# strict mode's no-implicit-reexport would flag legitimate imports/attribute access.
implicit_reexport = true

# pyarrow ships a partial py.typed, so when it's installed (dev env) mypy flags
# its untyped calls (e.g. ParquetFile), but it's absent from the slim CI
# typecheck deps. follow_imports = "skip" forces it to Any in *both* envs, so the
# code type-checks identically with or without pyarrow installed.
# Same treatment for the OTel span stack (tracing.py, A3), openlineage-python
# (lineage/emitter.py, #758), and numpy (datasources/comparison.py, #799): all
# typed (py.typed) but absent from the slim typecheck deps, so forcing them to
# Any keeps local + CI type-checking identical.
[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = ["pyarrow", "pyarrow.*", "numpy", "numpy.*", "opentelemetry.*", "azure.monitor.*", "openlineage.*"]
follow_imports = "skip"
ignore_missing_imports = true

# ── pytest ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
testpaths = ["backend/tests"]
# The Week-8 exit gate: the run fails if backend/app line coverage drops below
# 80% (baseline at flip time: 96%). Applies to every pytest run, local and CI.
# Use --no-cov for a quick single-file loop.
addopts = "--cov=backend/app --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=80"
markers = [
    "real_dispatch: opt out of the autouse run_dispatch stub (test dispatch_run itself)",
]
# Silence UPSTREAM deprecations we can't fix here — scoped by message so a new warning
# from our own code still surfaces. All resolve on a dependency upgrade; none are DataQ
# bugs. (Our one own use of the deprecated Starlette 422 constant was fixed in
# core/errors.py; the two opencensus datetime/locale ignores were dropped with the
# opencensus→OTel log migration, #524.)
filterwarnings = [
    "ignore:Using .httpx. with .starlette.testclient. is deprecated",                  # fastapi TestClient
    "ignore:Implicitly cleaning up <TemporaryDirectory:ResourceWarning",               # great_expectations ephemeral context
]

# ── Bandit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[tool.bandit]
targets = ["backend/app"]
exclude_dirs = ["backend/tests", "backend/alembic"]
skips = ["B101"]  # assert is fine in non-production code

# ── mutmut (mutation spikes) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Manual/periodic, NOT a CI gate (CONTRIBUTING rule 4a). mutmut 3.x copies the
# tree into ./mutants/, chdir's in, and runs pytest there — so `also_copy` must
# bring the whole importable package + tests + this config, and `-o addopts=`
# strips the repo's --cov flags (mutmut runs its own coverage plugin; a second
# --cov is a pytest usage error in-process). Per-spike: point `source_paths` at
# the one module you're spiking and narrow the test selection to its tests.
[tool.mutmut]
source_paths = [
    "backend/app/lineage/warehouse.py",
    "backend/app/lineage/warehouse_refresh.py",
    "backend/app/lineage/warehouse_snowflake.py",
    "backend/app/lineage/warehouse_unity_catalog.py",
]
also_copy = ["backend", "pyproject.toml"]
pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection = [
    "backend/tests/lineage/test_warehouse_snowflake.py",
    "backend/tests/lineage/test_warehouse_unity_catalog.py",
    "backend/tests/lineage/test_warehouse_refresh.py",
    "backend/tests/lineage/test_warehouse_connection_refresh.py",
]
pytest_add_cli_args = ["-o", "addopts="]

lines: # --cov is a pytest usage error in-process). Per-spike: point `source_paths` at
# the one module you're spiking and narrow the test selection to its tests.

input.command: rm -rf mutants && bash -c 'set -a; source .env >/dev/null 2>&1; set +a; export TEST_DATABASE_URL="REDACTED"; conda run -n dataq --no-capture-output mutmut run' 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Re-run mutmut on warehouse_refresh with test DB URL exported

attachment.snippet:
1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["setuptools>=83.0.0"]
3	build-backend = "setuptools.backends.legacy:build"
4	
5	# ── Black ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6	
7	[tool.black]
8	line-length = 100
9	target-version = ["py313"]
10	include = '\.pyi?$'
11	
12	# ── Ruff ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13	
14	[tool.ruff]
15	line-length = 100
16	target-version = "py313"
17	src = ["backend"]
18	
19	[tool.ruff.lint]
20	select = [
21	    "E",   # pycodestyle errors
22	    "W",   # pycodestyle warnings
23	    "F",   # pyflakes
24	    "I",   # isort
25	    "B",   # flake8-bugbear
26	    "C4",  # flake8-comprehensions
27	    "UP",  # pyupgrade
28	    "N",   # pep8-naming
29	    "S",   # flake8-bandit (security — basic subset; full scan via Bandit)
30	    "RUF", # ruff-specific rules
31	]
32	ignore = [
33	    "S101",  # assert statements are fine in tests
34	    "S104",  # binding to 0.0.0.0 is intentional in Docker
35	    "B008",  # FastAPI Depends() in function args is idiomatic
36	]
37	# The format-on-save hook runs `ruff check --fix`. Auto-deleting unused imports
38	# (F401) mid-edit — when an import is briefly unused between adding it and adding
39	# its first use — corrupts multi-step edits. Keep F401 *reported* (so genuinely
40	# unused imports still fail CI) but never auto-removed. Same for unused locals.
41	unfixable = ["F401", "F841"]
42	
43	[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
44	"backend/tests/**" = ["S", "N802"]  # relax security + naming in tests
45	"backend/alembic/**" = ["N999"]     # migration files use non-PEP8 names
46	
47	[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
48	known-first-party = ["app"]
49	
50	# ── mypy ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
51	
52	[tool.mypy]
53	python_version = "3.13"
54	strict = true
55	warn_return_any = true
56	warn_unused_configs = true
57	ignore_missing_imports = false
58	plugins = ["sqlalchemy.ext.mypy.plugin", "pydantic.mypy"]
59	
60	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
61	module = [
62	    "great_expectations.*",
63	    "celery.*",
64	    "snowflake.*",
65	    "databricks.*",
66	    "azure.storage.*",
67	    "azure.core.*",
68	    "azure.identity",
69	    "azure.keyvault.*",
70	    "boto3.*",
71	    "botocore.*",
72	    "pyiceberg.*",
73	    "pandas",
74	    "pandas.*",
75	    "croniter",
76	]
77	ignore_missing_imports = true
78	# These third-party libs expose public API via internal modules without an explicit
79	# __all__ (e.g. great_expectations.expectations.ExpectColumnValuesToNotBeNull), so
80	# strict mode's no-implicit-reexport would flag legitimate imports/attribute access.
81	implicit_reexport = true
82	
83	# pyarrow ships a partial py.typed, so when it's installed (dev env) mypy flags
84	# its untyped calls (e.g. ParquetFile), but it's absent from the slim CI
85	# typecheck deps. follow_imports = "skip" forces it to Any in *both* envs, so the
86	# code type-checks identically with or without pyarrow installed.
87	# Same treatment for the OTel span stack (tracing.py, A3), openlineage-python
88	# (lineage/emitter.py, #758), and numpy (datasources/comparison.py, #799): all
89	# typed (py.typed) but absent from the slim typecheck deps, so forcing them to
90	# Any keeps local + CI type-checking identical.
91	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
92	module = ["pyarrow", "pyarrow.*", "numpy", "numpy.*", "opentelemetry.*", "azure.monitor.*", "openlineage.*"]
93	follow_imports = "skip"
94	ignore_missing_imports = true
95	
96	# ── pytest ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
97	
98	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
99	asyncio_mode = "auto"
100	testpaths = ["backend/tests"]
101	# The Week-8 exit gate: the run fails if backend/app line coverage drops below
102	# 80% (baseline at flip time: 96%). Applies to every pytest run, local and CI.
103	# Use --no-cov for a quick single-file loop.
104	addopts = "--cov=backend/app --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=80"
105	markers = [
106	    "real_dispatch: opt out of the autouse run_dispatch stub (test dispatch_run itself)",
107	]
108	# Silence UPSTREAM deprecations we can't fix here — scoped by message so a new warning
109	# from our own code still surfaces. All resolve on a dependency upgrade; none are DataQ
110	# bugs. (Our one own use of the deprecated Starlette 422 constant was fixed in
111	# core/errors.py; the two opencensus datetime/locale ignores were dropped with the
112	# opencensus→OTel log migration, #524.)
113	filterwarnings = [
114	    "ignore:Using .httpx. with .starlette.testclient. is deprecated",                  # fastapi TestClient
115	    "ignore:Implicitly cleaning up <TemporaryDirectory:ResourceWarning",               # great_expectations ephemeral context
116	]
117	
118	# ── Bandit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
119	
120	[tool.bandit]
121	targets = ["backend/app"]
122	exclude_dirs = ["backend/tests", "backend/alembic"]
123	skips = ["B101"]  # assert is fine in non-production code
124	
125	# ── mutmut (mutation spikes) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
126	# Manual/periodic, NOT a CI gate (CONTRIBUTING rule 4a). mutmut 3.x copies the
127	# tree into ./mutants/, chdir's in, and runs pytest there — so `also_copy` must
128	# bring the whole importable package + tests + this config, and `-o addopts=`
129	# strips the repo's --cov flags (mutmut runs its own coverage plugin; a second
130	# --cov is a pytest usage error in-process). Per-spike: point `source_paths` at
131	# the one module you're spiking and narrow the test selection to its tests.
132	[tool.mutmut]
133	source_paths = ["backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py"]
134	also_copy = ["backend", "pyproject.toml"]
135	pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection = ["backend/tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py"]
136	pytest_add_cli_args = ["-o", "addopts="]

input.command:
gh issue create --title "test: mutation-spike findings (v1.1, #858 lineage + assets UI) — provider result-shape & edge-filter gaps; lineageLayout 66%" --label test --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Periodic rule-4a mutation spike, run 2026-07-17 on the newest code (#858 warehouse lineage backend + the assets frontend modules)

### mutmut — `backend/app/lineage/warehouse_refresh.py`
**236/239 killed (98.7%), 0 uncovered.** The 3 survivors are log-kwarg-only mutations (`error_type` dropped / `connection_id` blanked / the post-prune count's WHERE narrowed — all only change a log line). **No action.** Strongest module measured after `dashboard_service`.

### mutmut — `warehouse.py` + `warehouse_snowflake.py` + `warehouse_unity_catalog.py`
**261 killed / 142 survived on covered mutants (~65%).** Two survivor classes:

**Accepted (low-value, stub-seam):** the bulk are StringLiteral mutations of the static warehouse SQL (`OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` / `ACCESS_HISTORY` / `system.access.table_lineage` query text) and of degraded/skipped-reason strings — the unit tests drive stub connections that don't parse SQL, so these are unkillable without asserting exact query text (brittle; the queries were live-verified in the #858 session). One worthwhile middle ground: assert the UC query carries the `:since` **bind parameter** (parameterized watermark is contractual, injection-relevant).

**Real gaps (suggested tests):**
1. `_from_object_dependencies`: `continue`→`break` survives twice — a FUNCTION/PROCEDURE endpoint (or NULL-db row) **mid-stream** would silently truncate every remaining edge. Add a fixture with a function-domain row *between* two table rows. Also `or`→`and` on the `_TABLE_DOMAINS` guard.
2. `_from_access_history`: `and`→`or` on the `up is not None and down is not None` guard survives — a half-parseable row could emit a malformed edge. Also `namespace=None` threading into `_identity_from_qualified` survives.
3. `fetch_edges` success path: `tier=None`, `skipped_tiers=None`, and dropped kwargs on the GET_LINEAGE-tier `WarehouseLineageResult` all survive — the ladder tests assert descent but not the **result shape** of the top-tier success (tier is user-visible on the asset graph).
4. `_account_has_write_activity`: `> 0`→`> 1` survives (count==1 boundary); `_sqlstate`: the `.orig` attribute walk is untested (wrap a fake DBAPI error with `.orig.sqlstate`).
5. UC `_namespace`: the no-scheme `path.split("/", 1)` fallback mutants survive — add a `host/path` workspace_url case.

### Stryker — `frontend/src/components/assets/{assetTree,health,lineageLayout,namespaceLabel}.ts`
**81.84% overall (293 killed · 57 survived · 8 no-cov · 0 error).** Per file: assetTree 83.6 · health 88.1 · **lineageLayout 66.3 (26 survivors — worst)** · namespaceLabel 85.9.

Real gaps:
- `health.ts`: `if (false)` survives on the `has_active_run` → running and `last_run_at` → Reachable guards — the **connection-axis state ladder** isn't asserted branch-by-branch; plus `color: ""` survivors on the 'No runs' entries (same colour-map-as-contract class as #563 — table-driven colour assertions fix both).
- `assetTree.ts`: the sibling **sort comparator** (`label` then `namespace` localeCompare) mutates to `true`/`false`/`() => undefined` with tests green — ordering is unasserted.
- `lineageLayout.ts` (66%): node-flag survivors incl. `isMonitored: false` **on the center node** — layout output flags/coordinates largely unasserted.
- `namespaceLabel.ts`: `HOST_IS_THE_CATALOG` scheme-set entries mutate to `""` unkilled; `namespace.trim()` removal survives; `path.length > 0`→`>= 0` boundary.

### Harness gotcha (worth knowing for every future spike)
mutmut 3 copies the tree into `mutants/` **without the repo-root `.env`**, so DB-backed tests skip silently during stats collection and every mutant in a DB-covered module reports **"no tests"** (this spike's first pass showed 239/642 uncovered for exactly this reason). Fix: export `TEST_DATABASE_URL` (built from the `.env` Postgres creds, pointing at `dataq_test`) before `mutmut run`. With it set, the same module went 0%→98.7% measured.

Spike configs were reverted after the run (per-spike `[tool.mutmut]` targeting per the pyproject comment); Stryker ran via `frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate '...assets/*.ts'`.
EOF
)"

2	 {
3	  "RuleID": "jwt",
4	  "Description": "Uncovered a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive user data.",
5	  "StartLine": 36,
6	  "EndLine": 36,
7	  "StartColumn": 10,
8	  "EndColumn": 111,
9	  "Match": "REDACTED"",
10	  "Secret": "REDACTED",
11	  "Attributes": {
12	   "git.author_email": "royarijit1995@gmail.com",
13	   "git.author_name": "Arijit Roy",
14	   "git.date": "2026-07-13T05:58:56Z",
15	   "git.message": "fix(security): stop logging DataQ PATs in plaintext (#849)

Found live in prod App Insights:

    DecodeError: Malformed token received. dq_live_NNZ5… Error: Not enough segments

`fastapi_azure_auth` logs the RAW token when a JWT decode fails (auth.py:171).
A DataQ PAT is not a JWT, so it fails that decode by construction — and
`Security(azure_scheme)` is a FastAPI dependency, so it resolves BEFORE
get_current_user's body. The "PAT is tried first by prefix" ordering documented
in core/auth.py was therefore never actually first: every PAT-authenticated
request handed the token to a JWT validator, which logged the plaintext
credential at WARNING (plus an exception record), and only then fell through to
the PAT path — which succeeded, so nothing looked wrong.

A PAT is a live bearer credential. Anyone with read access to App Insights could
lift one and authenticate as its owner — including the workspace-admin PAT.

Two layers, both needed:

1. Remove the cause: _PatAwareAzureScheme short-circuits to None when the bearer
   carries the PAT prefix, so a PAT never reaches a JWT validator.

2. Logger-level backstop: core.logging now scrubs bare bearer credentials
   (dq_live_…, JWTs, `Bearer <token>`) from log strings. The existing scrubber
   only caught `key=value` query params and URL userinfo, so a BARE token in a
   message body sailed straight through.

Layer 2 is the architectural point: the call site that leaked was NOT OURS.
CLAUDE.md §10 already says redact at the logger, not the call site — this is
exactly why. We cannot audit what every dependency chooses to log, and grepping
our own code for "places we log tokens" would never have found this, because we
don't log it: a dependency does.

The PAT-short-circuit test intercepts the LIBRARY's own __call__, not our return
value — the buggy version also returned None (after logging the credential), so a
test that only checked the return value would have passed on the bug.

Both prod PATs must be rotated AFTER this deploys (re-minting before then would
just leak the new one).

Fixes #849
