2. Logger-level backstop: core.logging now scrubs bare bearer credentials
   (dq_live_…, JWTs, `Bearer <token>`) from log strings. The existing scrubber
   only caught `key=value` query params and URL userinfo, so a BARE token in a
   message body sailed straight through.

Layer 2 is the architectural point: the call site that leaked was NOT OURS.
CLAUDE.md §10 already says redact at the logger, not the call site — this is
exactly why. We cannot audit what every dependency chooses to log, and grepping
our own code for "places we log tokens" would never have found this, because we
don't log it: a dependency does.

The PAT-short-circuit test intercepts the LIBRARY's own __call__, not our return
value — the buggy version also returned None (after logging the credential), so a
test that only checked the return value would have passed on the bug.

Both prod PATs must be rotated AFTER this deploys (re-minting before then would
just leak the new one).

Fixes #849

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 55202ed13ab2"
 }
]

per-seed traversal deferred → **[#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)**), ACCESS_HISTORY **empty-corroborated** against QUERY_HISTORY (silently empty on Standard — a catch would mislabel it), OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES the live-verified floor; review fix — `skipped_tiers` carries the real exception message, not a hardcoded "unsupported on this edition" that was wrong for Enterprise. **[#887](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/887)** Unity Catalog provider (`is_incremental=True` → incremental `event_time` watermark, **never prune**, since `system.access.table_lineage` is an append-only log) with a **6h safety re-scan window** so ingestion-lag rows landing after the watermark aren't lost (idempotent upsert makes the re-read harmless); the watermark never regresses below `since`. **[#890](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/890)** the daily beat task (`refresh_warehouse_lineage`, **dark by default** — `warehouse_lineage_enabled=False`) + a backward-compatible migration (`960d18679639`, 5 nullable additive columns) persisting `lineage_watermark`/`last_refresh_at`/`last_tier`/`degraded_reason`/`last_error` per connection; the recorded reason is **classified, never raw** (same discipline as #828's poll-health). **[#891](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/891)** surfaces tier/degraded/failing on the asset graph — an **INFO**-level advisory (distinct from the failing-source **WARNING**) reading the stored `degraded_reason` / `last_error`, gated behind the same asset-stake rule as `failing_lineage_sources`. Providers are tested against **real captured payloads** (byte-for-byte identity pins per #823, query-column projections committed / full captures gitignored). Two process notes banked to memory: a `mypy (tests)` gate miss (CI runs mypy over `backend/tests` too — #418 — a `str | None` used with `in` without an `is not None` narrow cost one round-trip on #891); and the live-warehouse reads stayed spike-parked (Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25), so **[#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893)** — verify e2e against a deployed warehouse — is filed. **Dependabot batch cleared + the #858 spike ran (user-approved):** all 12 version bumps (#860–#871) merged — the stale branches predated the #878 audit fix, and #861 (mypy 2.1→2.3) needed a manual companion commit aligning the pre-commit hook rev that Dependabot can't touch (the `typecheck-deps-sync` gate would have failed it); mypy 2.3.0 verified clean repo-wide first. CodeQL alert 49 fixed ([#884](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/884) — the deadline-thread helper now records-and-RE-RAISES BaseException, keeping the no-masquerade property compliantly). **#858 spike findings** (issue comments have the full detail): real payloads captured from BOTH warehouses (redacted, parked untracked in `backend/tests/fixtures/lineage_native/` for the build PR) — UC `system.access.table_lineage`/`column_lineage` **work on Free Edition** with `event_time` as the watermark and reads-as-NULL-target rows to filter; Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` captured with UPPER identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`; **`ACCESS_HISTORY` is silently EMPTY on Standard** (45k QUERY_HISTORY rows, 0 ACCESS_HISTORY — the preflight must corroborate, not catch); **`GET_LINEAGE` errors cleanly (0A000) on Standard** → the build's source ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, preferring the clean-error preflight. Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25; captures are banked so the build no longer depends on the window.) (**2026-07-17 — `schema_drift` SHIPPED end-to-end ([#881](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/881) engine + [#883](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/883) UI):** the first stateful monitor kind — one registry entry (#880's seam held: zero chain edits), a session-aware baseline-diff executor (the #794 comparison pattern; runners never see stateful kinds), per-datasource introspection (SQL via one bound-param `information_schema` query, flat-file via Parquet footer / bounded CSV sample, **Iceberg from table metadata — closing #859's drift leg**), `metric_value` = drifted-column count under ADR-0016 bands, dry-run preview that never writes, and the edit-gated re-baseline action (drop-the-row, recapture-next-run — never introspection on the API thread). The review caught two shippers: concurrent first runs both inserting the baseline — the loser's IntegrityError failed the WHOLE run and discarded sibling results (now `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and `UPPER()` information_schema matching MERGING two case-variant tables into a union schema no real table has (now exact-spelling-wins + classified 'ambiguous' refusal). Honest-limits documented: CSV dtype-inference noise (ignore_columns/re-baseline is the workaround) and whole-blob Parquet reads (range-read filed as [#882](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/882)). Two CI-only failures taught local-gate lessons now in memory: `pnpm lint` ≠ `tsc`, and version-dependent library inference must never anchor a fixture.) (**2026-07-16 — the W4 schema-drift prep chain shipped in one session:** #427 one-engine-per-runner ([#875](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/875) — review added `LazyEngine`, `pool_pre_ping` for the mixed-suite stale-connection regression, and the `owned_runner` lifecycle) · #592 slice 1, the `monitor_baselines` diff-reference store ([#876](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/876) — migration-safety review caught a missing `gen_random_uuid()` id default that only a real INSERT could surface) · #726+#429 monitor-kind strategy registry + runner-advertised capability gate ([#880](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/880)) · the #859 Iceberg metadata fast-paths ([#879](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/879) — review caught that snapshot `total-records` never nets row-level deletes: a merge-on-read table would have false-PASSED a volume floor; both metadata paths now require PROOF of zero deletes). **CI-wide outage fixed same session (#877/[#878](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/878)):** npm retired the legacy audit endpoints 2026-07-15 (410) and no released pnpm speaks the bulk endpoint (verified vs 11.13.1) — the required audit gate now queries the bulk advisory endpoint directly over the same lockfile graph, proven to FAIL on known-vulnerable versions and fail-closed on parse/endpoint drift; #877 stays open for the revert-to-pnpm-audit condition. The #858 warehouse-native lineage spike is consent-blocked on live prod reads.) (**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. ADR 0035 + deploy/README synced, incl. the new Redis SCAN pattern `rl:webhook:*:ip:*`.) (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.) (**user follow-up batch #801–#805 all closed 2026-07-12** — the five UI/ops follow-ups filed off the ADR-0015 session: mobile overlay nav ([#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817)), hierarchical asset browse ([#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818)), asset-health split into connection-vs-data-quality axes ([#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819)), the left-to-right lineage graph ([#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820)), and the Marquez local verification + runbook correction ([#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821)). Four latent bugs surfaced and were fixed en route: a run that evaluated nothing read as a green "Passing"; a `cancelled` run rolled up green on **both** health axes; an asset→asset navigation showed the *previous* asset's data (nothing could reach it before the graph's clickable nodes); and the demo seed had **no lineage at all**, so a fresh install could not show the feature. **#813 closed via [#814](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/814)** — weekly `/security-scan` OWASP spot-check found the MCP `create_check`/`update_check` tools had no Pydantic layer, so `name`/`expectation_type` could exceed the REST 256/128 bounds and hit a raw Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` instead of a clean 422; pushed `check_service.validate_lengths` (now public) into both, extended to `suite_io_service.import_suite`'s direct `Check(...)` construction too (agentic review caught that same-class gap pre-merge). Same session: 13 open CodeQL code-scanning alerts triaged — 10 dismissed as false-positive/by-design (module-level cache globals, a documented lazy-import cycle, an intentional test canary), 3 genuine (`side-effect-in-assert` ×2, `unnecessary-lambda`) fixed in [#815](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/815). **#799 comparison:columns + tolerance + optimization shipped (this PR)** — FDC's column grain as `comparison:columns` (per-column matched/mismatched/additional-per-side VALUE buckets, dropna parity, same sample shape → redaction/report reuse), numeric `config.tolerance` {absolute, relative} on both grains (the #808-review float32 finding — one-sided NULLs never tolerance-equal), and the shared keys+positions alignment pass (×1.52 on the 1M-row benchmark, bucket parity asserted; integer keys now render "1" not "1.0" in samples); catalog fixture regenerated, per-column breakdown + tolerance fields in the UI. · **ADR [0015](adr/0015-two-connection-comparison-check-model.md) written + merged ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790), 2026-07-12)** — the two-connection comparison check model: the suite stays single-connection and supplies the **target under test**; a `comparison` check adds one **source** ref. Closes the design gate reserved since ADR 0014; build set [#791](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/791)–[#795](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/795) filed on W3. **#795 comparison UI + report download shipped (this PR) — the ADR 0015 build is COMPLETE (#791–#795, five PRs in one session 2026-07-12)**: side-by-side check editor (source pane = connection picker + table-or-SQL per type + keys; target pane locked to the suite's connection — the §1 invariant made visible; common options below), RunDetail bucket counts + redacted sample tables + on-demand CSV/XLSX report download (derived server-side from the redacted buckets via new `comparison_report.py` + `openpyxl` (MIT, pinned), never stored), comparison-safe CheckEdit (name+thresholds only), catalog entry + regenerated GX-contract fixture, feature-matrix row; Playwright comparison spec green against the live local stack (which also re-validated #797 — the stale api container 429'd the suite until recreated). **#794 comparison run path shipped ([#809](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/809))** — `services/comparison_run.py` builds a per-run executor bound to the suite's resolved target (source side = the check's ref + `config.source` through `resolve_target`/batch materialization or a query spec; target side = the run's materialized table or `config.target_query`), reads both via the #792 reader, diffs with the #793 engine, and maps buckets → `CheckOutcome` (mismatch-% → `metric_value` under ADR 0016 banding; counts → `observed_value`; capped samples → `sample_failures`). `_run_outcomes` routes comparison checks to the injected executor (None → per-check `error`, callers stay safe); worker builds it only when the suite has comparison checks. Failure semantics #122: reader/engine refusals + unexpected exceptions → operational `error` results (classified messages, never raw text). The sample redactor gains a comparison branch — suffix-stripped policy matching so `pii_columns: [email]` masks `email_src`+`email_tgt`, unknown columns keep default-mask. **#793 comparison engine shipped ([#808](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/808))** — `datasources/comparison.py`, the FDC record-grain diff port (ADR 0014 §2: engine yes, app no): key-joined outer-merge buckets (matched / mismatched / additional-in-source / additional-in-target) with per-column mismatch counts, typed `DuplicateKeyError` (FDC silently drop_duplicates-ed — pairing must never be ambiguous) + `ComparisonInputError`, NA-safe nullable-`string` comparison (null==null matches; a literal "nan" ≠ NULL — fixes the FDC astype(str) quirk), pairwise numeric canonicalization (numpy NULL-int→float64 "10.0" vs Arrow "10" can't fabricate mismatches; dup-key detection on normalized keys, samples echo original values), mismatch-% badness scalar over the logical-row union, capped JSON-clean sample buckets. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped ([#807](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/807))** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). User follow-up batch filed to `v1.1 Backlog` (2026-07-12): [#799](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/799) comparison:columns + optimization pass · [#800](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/800) Astronomer verification · [#801](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/801) mobile overlay nav · [#802](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/802) hierarchical asset browse · [#803](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/803) asset health split · [#804](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/804) Marquez in harness · [#805](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/805) lineage graph view · [#806](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/806) stale-nosec chore. Same session: **#796 CI-E2E 429 regression fixed via [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797)** — the #783 limiter throttled the tokenless dev-bypass Playwright suite (whole suite = one per-IP `unauth` bucket); diagnosed from the report artifact ("Too many requests" rendered in the failing pages); `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false` in the CI E2E job + compose dev default. · **#725 request rate limiting shipped (PR #783)** — app-level *innermost* HTTP middleware on every public surface (REST + orchestration webhooks + the `/mcp` mount a route dependency can't reach); fixed-window Redis counter (window index in the key → no EXPIRE race), **no new dep** (`redis` INCR over slowapi/limits), **fail-open**; endpoint classes `webhook`(per-IP) · `default`(per-`sha256(token)`) · `unauth`(per-IP), raw token never keyed/logged; `/healthz`+`OPTIONS` exempt; 429 carries `Retry-After` + `detail.retry_after_seconds`; ADR [0035](adr/0035-request-rate-limiting.md); the named hard prereq for the ADR-0032 OTP sign-in (#738). · **#773 nav inversion shipped (this PR)** — G-d phase 4: the dashboard opens with an asset-health lead strip (monitored / need-attention / in-progress, severity-sorted preview, explicit first-200 truncation note), Assets moves to the top of the sidebar, suite/run detail link back to their asset, and admins get an owner-reassignment picker on AssetDetail (mount-gated so the /me-resolution race can't strand it; `/admin/users` never fetched for non-admins — structural + test-pinned). 1	title = "DataQ betterleaks config"
2	
3	# The global `filter` MUST be a top-level key, BEFORE [extend] — otherwise TOML
4	# scopes it under [extend] and betterleaks silently ignores it. A finding is
5	# skipped when this expression evaluates true (betterleaks discard-on-true).
6	#
7	# Skip ONLY Entire (entire.io) session-transcript checkpoints. Two path shapes:
8	#   - checkpoint shard commits:  <2hex>/<hex>/<seq>/full.jsonl  (e.g. c1/c6b5fdf0cb/0/full.jsonl)
9	#   - .entire metadata dir:      .entire/.../full.jsonl
10	# These are agent session logs that capture raw tool output (public download-URL
11	# hashes, sha256 digests, git SHAs) which trip high-entropy / 40-hex rules such as
12	# `sourcegraph-access-token` (e.g. the SWA CLI's StaticSitesClient@<40hex> URL).
13	# They live on an ephemeral checkpoint ref and never merge to main.
14	#
15	# Anchored to those exact shapes so a stray src/full.jsonl or evilfull.jsonl is
16	# still fully scanned. Verified (betterleaks v1.4.1, git mode): real secrets in
17	# normal files AND in src/full.jsonl / evilfull.jsonl are still reported; only the
18	# two checkpoint shapes are filtered; the default ruleset stays active (extend).
19	# Also verified the public checkpoint ref holds NO real credentials — only these
20	# hash false positives — so this filter hides false positives, not secrets.
21	filter = 'attributes[?"path"].orValue("").matches("^([0-9a-f]{2}/[0-9a-f]+/[0-9]+|\\.entire/.*)/full\\.jsonl$")'
22	
23	[extend]
24	useDefault = true

file.content:
title = "DataQ betterleaks config"

# The global `filter` MUST be a top-level key, BEFORE [extend] — otherwise TOML
# scopes it under [extend] and betterleaks silently ignores it. A finding is
# skipped when this expression evaluates true (betterleaks discard-on-true).
#
# Skip ONLY Entire (entire.io) session-transcript checkpoints. Two path shapes:
#   - checkpoint shard commits:  <2hex>/<hex>/<seq>/full.jsonl  (e.g. c1/c6b5fdf0cb/0/full.jsonl)
#   - .entire metadata dir:      .entire/.../full.jsonl
# These are agent session logs that capture raw tool output (public download-URL
# hashes, sha256 digests, git SHAs) which trip high-entropy / 40-hex rules such as
# `sourcegraph-access-token` (e.g. the SWA CLI's StaticSitesClient@<40hex> URL).
# They live on an ephemeral checkpoint ref and never merge to main.
#
# Anchored to those exact shapes so a stray src/full.jsonl or evilfull.jsonl is
# still fully scanned. Verified (betterleaks v1.4.1, git mode): real secrets in
# normal files AND in src/full.jsonl / evilfull.jsonl are still reported; only the
# two checkpoint shapes are filtered; the default ruleset stays active (extend).
# Also verified the public checkpoint ref holds NO real credentials — only these
# hash false positives — so this filter hides false positives, not secrets.
filter = 'attributes[?"path"].orValue("").matches("^([0-9a-f]{2}/[0-9a-f]+/[0-9]+|\\.entire/.*)/full\\.jsonl$")'

[extend]
useDefault = true

content.content:
scan git repositories for secrets

Usage:
  betterleaks git [flags] [repo]

Flags:
      --git-workers int   number of parallel git log workers (0 = single process)
  -h, --help              help for git
      --log-opts string   git log options
      --platform string   the target platform used to generate links (github, gitlab)
      --pre-commit        scan using git diff
      --staged            scan staged commits (good for pre-commit)

Global Flags:
  -b, --baseline-path string          path to baseline with issues that can be ignored
  -c, --config string                 config file path
                                      order of precedence:
                                      1. --config/-c
                                      2. env var BETTERLEAKS_CONFIG or GITLEAKS_CONFIG
                                      3. env var BETTERLEAKS_CONFIG_TOML or GITLEAKS_CONFIG_TOML with the file content
                                      4. (target path)/.betterleaks.toml or .gitleaks.toml
                                      If none of the four options are used, then the default config will be used
      --diagnostics string            enable diagnostics (http OR comma-separated list: cpu,mem,trace). cpu=CPU prof, mem=memory prof, trace=exec tracing, http=serve via net/http/pprof
      --diagnostics-dir string        directory to store diagnostics output files when not using http mode (defaults to current directory)
      --enable-rule strings           only enable specific rules by id
      --exit-code int                 exit code when leaks have been encountered (default 1)
      --experiments string            comma-separated list of experimental features to enable
  -i, --gitleaks-ignore-path string   path to .betterleaksignore or .gitleaksignore file or folder containing one (default ".")
      --ignore-gitleaks-allow         ignore gitleaks:allow and betterleaks:allow comments
      --legacy-print                  use legacy key/value verbose finding format (requires --verbose)
  -l, --log-level string              log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal) (default "info")
      --match-context string          context around match: L (lines), C (columns/characters). e.g. 10L, 100C, -2C,+4C
      --max-archive-depth int         allow scanning into nested archives up to this depth (default "0", no archive traversal is done)
      --max-decode-depth int          allow recursive decoding up to this depth (default 5)
      --max-target-megabytes int      files larger than this will be skipped
      --no-banner                     suppress banner
      --no-color                      turn off color for verbose output
      --redact uint[=100]             redact secrets from logs and stdout. To redact only parts of the secret just apply a percent value from 0..100. For example --redact=20 (default 100%)
      --regex-engine string           regex engine (stdlib, re2) (default "re2")
  -f, --report-format string          output format (json, csv, junit, sarif, template)

input.content:
# Fingerprints of triaged full-history scan findings (betterleaks/gitleaks format:
# <commit>:<path>:<rule>:<line>). CI's incremental scan never re-visits these old
# blobs; this file keeps the periodic FULL-history scan (security cadence, CONTRIBUTING

---
name: project-azure-winddown
description: "Azure free-trial credit exhausted — subscription DISABLED 2026-07-12 (~2 weeks earlier than the planned ~2026-07-26 wind-down); all dataq-rg resources down"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 0aef84ae-9324-4eaf-a425-b6451fedf029
---

**UPDATE 2026-07-12: the subscription is now DISABLED — the wind-down happened on its
own, early and hard.** User got the "$200 credit used up, account and services disabled"
email same day. Verified via `az rest .../subscriptions/{id}?api-version=2022-12-01`:
`.state == "Disabled"` (`quotaId: FreeTrial_2014-09-01`, `spendingLimit: On` — no PAYG
upgrade, so the spending limit hard-stopped everything instead of billing). Confirmed
dead, not a stale CLI cache: Postgres `dataq-pg-wus3-3erlgd` shows `state: Disabled`;
every Container App in `dataq-rg` (dataq-app-api/worker/frontend/redis + the 3 harness
apps) shows `provisioningState: Failed`; the live frontend URL times out completely (no
response, not even a 401). **Gotcha:** `az account show` / `az account list` still
reported `state: Enabled` — those two commands are not reliable for judging subscription
health; use the `az rest` subscription-resource check above, or just try to reach a
resource directly.

**How to apply:** treat DataQ's Azure deployment as gone until the user decides to either
(a) add a payment method in the portal to convert to PAYG and restore, or (b) accept this
as the local-first cutover (#590/#591) landing ~2 weeks early. Don't propose Azure-
dependent work (live smoke tests, prod deploys, KV secret reads, the harness wake
runbook) without first re-checking subscription state via the `az rest` call — it may
still be Disabled. As of 2026-07-12 the user has only asked for a status check, no
restore/wind-down action taken yet. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] and
[[project-dbt-lineage-harness]] for what lived on this subscription.

---

Original 2026-07-04 wind-down plan (superseded above, kept for the cost-actuals context —
the harness stop/mockdata-suspend steps below are accurate history of what was already
off before the hard disablement):

Decision (2026-07-04): **no pay-as-you-go upgrade** — DataQ wraps up its Azure presence by
the free-trial day-30 mark (**~2026-07-26**; W3 milestone due 2026-07-25 is the hard
deadline for #590 teardown + #591 local-first posture). Snowflake subscription lapses
separately ~2026-07-05..10 (v1.1 W1, #588).

**Why:** Cost Management actuals (billing currency **CAD**): burn was ~21.7/day
(harness = 80% — Airflow scheduler 8.99 + 2×2-vCPU workers 6.92/day); CAD 144.80 of the
free credit already consumed by Jul 4 → credit would have died ~Jul 9–10. Postgres
B1ms/32GB is $0 (12-month free SKU). App-only burn ~CAD 4.5/day.

**State since 2026-07-04:** the 4 harness container apps (`dataq-harness-airflow`,
`-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis`) are **Stopped**; both ADF triggers
(`tr_customers_daily`, `tr_orders_landed`) **Stopped**; 5 `mockdata-*` cron jobs
**suspended** (api-version `2024-08-02-preview`). The 4 `dataq-app-*` apps still run.
Flows B/C dead until woken; DataQ's 10-min Airflow poll logs failures (noise only).
**Wake runbook is scripted: `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh`**
(`status|start|run [--adf]|stop|window [--adf]`; ~CAD 17/day ≈ 0.70/hour while awake;
`--adf` needs live Snowflake). Also mirrored as a #590 comment.
**Snowflake timeline corrected 2026-07-04:** the trial ends ~2026-07-25 (21 days,
$384 credits left) — same window as Azure, NOT "within days". The #588 retirement was
executed then reversed the same day; the real retirement re-runs at actual lapse with
#590. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] for the reversal state (fresh 25-day
PAT; schedules/bindings restore still pending — needs dataq-admin).

**How to apply:** don't treat stopped harness apps / failing Airflow polls as incidents;
before any live E2E, wake per the runbook and stop again after. `az` CLI: was left logged
in as demo user Olivia after go-live; owner login is royarijit04@outlook.com (relevant to
[[project-live-connections-and-demo]] credential hygiene at teardown).

input.description: Read harness window script header

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
#
# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
#
# Usage:
#   harness_window.sh status                  # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start                    # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf] [--dags] [--dbt] [--iceberg]
#                                              # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
#                                              #   --adf:  manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*
#                                              #   --iceberg: resume+start the iceberg-writer ACA job (Flow D — appends
#                                              #      a batch to the Iceberg table retail.purchase_orders via pyiceberg),
#                                              #      wait, re-suspend. NO Snowflake dep (like --dags); DataQ reads the
#                                              #      table natively on its own schedule (no trigger-binding).
#                                              #   --dbt:  resume+start the dbt-lineage ACA job (#609 — dbt build
#                                              #      staging views + mart dynamic tables, artifacts to ADLS), wait,
#                                              #      re-suspend. Needs live Snowflake (like --adf). Runs LAST so a
#                                              #      same-window --adf/--dags load is what the marts refresh from.
#                                              #   --dags: manual-trigger the 3 cron DAGs via the Airflow REST API
#                                              #     (POST /api/v1/dags/{id}/dagRuns, basic auth; unpauses first --
#                                              #      a run created on a paused DAG never executes). Needs the
#                                              #      harness awake. flow_a_payments_event is NOT force-triggered:
#                                              #      it requires an Event Grid conf payload and fires naturally
#                                              #      when the mockdata files land while the harness is awake.
#                                              #      flow_a_snowflake_load fails after the Snowflake lapse (#588).
#   harness_window.sh stop                     # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf] [--dags] [--dbt] [--iceberg]  # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# Job semantics: a suspended ACA job REFUSES manual starts, so `run` resumes each
# job, starts the execution, and suspends again at the end — everything executes
# on demand, never waiting for (or leaving armed) the cron schedules. `run` works
# standalone (mockdata jobs have no dependency on the Airflow apps).
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
TF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../terraform" && pwd)"   # harness TF holds the Airflow admin creds
DAGS=(flow_a_snowflake_load flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion)     # cron DAGs; payments_event is event-only

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
# Marquez (the ADR 0034 LineageProvider catalog, #804) rides the same window: the
# dbt job emits OpenLineage into it (`dbt-ol`, only when OPENLINEAGE_URL is set) and
# DataQ pulls the graph back out into its own `lineage_edges` cache. It is started
# FIRST (so the dbt emit has somewhere to land) and stopped LAST — and stopping it
# is safe: the pulled edges are cached in DataQ's DB, so the lineage graph in the UI
# survives the catalog going away, and a dead catalog is treated as *unavailable*

content.text:
Deploy is running (backend build/migrate/deploy job in progress). Status while I wait on the two background tasks (deploy watch + betterleaks allowlist verification):

**Done so far:**
- **Mutation spikes** — backend: `warehouse_refresh.py` **236/239 killed (98.7%)** once the DB-skip harness artifact was fixed; the Snowflake/UC lineage providers ~65% on covered mutants with real assertion gaps triaged; frontend: **81.8%** across the four assets modules (worst: `lineageLayout.ts` 66%). All findings filed as [#898](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/898), including the `TEST_DATABASE_URL` gotcha for future spikes.
- **Security scan** — Bandit clean, pip-audit clean, pnpm audit clean (the transient "1 moderate" was Stryker's temporary dev-deps), full-history betterleaks found exactly one benign historical fixture (jwt.io demo token in the #849 test's first version); targeted review of the #849/#852 fix classes verified clean (logger-level scrub incl. exported attrs + traceback dicts, `azure.core` filter + no-propagate, span URLs stripped to path, classified-never-raw error persistence in the new lineage code, prod-docs gate holding).
- **Docs** — current through #897; one nit: the deploy runbook's `$FE/openapi.json → 404` probe expectation is stale since the SPA catch-all (real gate is `/api/v1/openapi.json → 404`, which holds).
- **Deploy pre-checklist** — all green; workflow `29622325290` dispatched on SHA `4f8b2ae0`.

Next: post-deploy smoke (probes + infra roll + DB-truth check), one harness `window` run for flow validation, then confirm everything is back asleep.

input.command:
git checkout -b chore/betterleaks-history-allowlist && git add .betterleaksignore && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(security): allowlist the triaged historical jwt fixture in full-history secret scans

The periodic full-history betterleaks scan (security cadence) flags one finding:
the #849 redaction test's ORIGINAL fixture literals in commit 08d2bbd3 — the
public jwt.io demo token (a documentation value, not a credential) next to a
fabricated-value PAT stand-in. A later commit already replaced both with
constructed strings; the literals survive only in that historical blob, which
cannot be purged (force-push to main is blocked by the ruleset).

Add a .betterleaksignore carrying that finding's fingerprint so full-history
scans report zero and a real leak can never hide behind a known false positive.
CI's incremental scan is unaffected (it never re-visits the old blob).

Verified with betterleaks v1.4.1 in git mode over the full 6.75 GB history:
1 finding before, "no leaks found" after; the fingerprint pins commit+path+
rule+line, so any NEW secret — even in the same file — still reports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -u origin chore/betterleaks-history-allowlist 2>&1 | tail -2
input.description: Branch, commit, push the betterleaks allowlist
